purpose: Define the app-facing ordinary Bible book file shape for multiple translations while preserving compatibility with the existing scalar NRSV corpus during migration.

appliesTo: ordinary chapter/verse Bible book files under data/bible
future full-Bible translation imports such as Rotherham
doesNotReplace: consolidated psalter list schema in data/bible/OT/psalms.json
governed non-chapter ET collection and hymn schemas

currentCompatibilityState.currentOrdinaryBookShape: single-version book object with meta.version and scalar verse.text
resolverBehavior: scalar verse.text remains valid and is returned as-is
object verse.text selects requested translation key
missing requested translation falls back to NRSV
missing NRSV falls back to first string-valued translation in the text object

migrationRules: Do not create one book tree per translation as the app-facing runtime model.
Use one canonical file per biblical book, with translation keys under verse.text.
Preserve current scalar verse.text until a book is intentionally migrated.
When migrating a book, move existing scalar text into verse.text.NRSV without changing text content.
Add new translations under explicit source-named keys such as Rotherham, not theological category names.
Do not import a translation without a governed source witness or reproducible source-intake workflow.
Run npm run audit:bible-corpus-structure before and after any migration/import tranche.
Do not assume every translation exists for every book or every extra-canonical lane.
rotherhamRules: Treat Rotherham as a full-Bible side project where source coverage permits.
Start with a bounded pilot only after source witness intake and book-level migration rules are implemented.
Do not import Rotherham by hardcoding long biblical text inside temporary patch scripts.
openQuestionsBeforeImport: Where will governed Rotherham source witness files live?
Will source intake be committed as raw source, normalized source, or both?
Which single book should be the pilot import after Psalms/source testing?
How should UI expose translation selection without disrupting existing offices?
